summary: Phase-6 Buyer → Search Seller flow (backend search endpoint + new add-seller search UI + new seller-details screen) was tested end-to-end on the public preview URL. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases pass (auth 401/403, empty/whitespace q → empty, partial+case-insensitive matching on businessName, sellerCode, address, pincode, exclusion of self, no _id leak, connectionStatus annotation null→Pending after POST, 409 duplicate with 'Connection already exists (status: Pending)' detail). Frontend: full happy-path verified — buyer login → tap Appbar search icon (search-seller-btn) → /buyer-add-seller renders with seller-search-input → debounced search returns the seeded UITEST_GreenLeaf_KMHHB card with sellerCode chip, pincode chip and short address (NO Connect button on card) → 'No sellers found' empty-title shown for a zzz-query → tap card → /buyer-seller-details renders full address, owner, mobile, Open availability chip → Send Connection Request button enabled → tap → label flips to 'Request Pending', aria-disabled=true, helper text 'Your connection request is awaiting the seller's approval.', Pending chip in details, and on returning to search the same card now shows a Pending chip and re-opening details keeps the button disabled with 'Request Pending'. Search by full sellerCode, lowercase sellerCode and pincode (560038) all resolve the seeded seller. One UX nit (not a bug): /(buyer)/home.tsx line 124 empty-state hint still reads 'Tap the + button to connect using a Seller Code.' which is stale copy for the new unified-search flow.

issues: Empty-state hint at line 124 reads 'Tap the + button to connect using a Seller Code.' — stale copy since the connect flow is no longer a single-code field. Suggest: 'Tap the search icon or + to find sellers by code, name, address or pincode.'

action_items: Optional copy update on (buyer)/home.tsx line 124 to mention search-by-name/address/pincode instead of only 'Seller Code'.

Optional: ensure the search-result Card's testID is placed on the outer Pressable wrapper so RNW-on-Playwright can click the card center directly (currently Playwright must click an inner text element to dispatch the press). Functional on real devices — only impacts web-automation ergonomics.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/buyer/sellers/search escapes the user input via re.escape(...) before building the $regex — safe against regex injection.
Bulk-fetches existing buyer→seller connections by sellerId $in [...] then builds an O(1) sellerId→connection map for annotation — avoids N+1 queries.
Hard cap of .limit(50) on the sellers cursor is sensible but unsorted; for deterministic UX consider sorting by businessName/sellerCode. Not a correctness bug.
Mongo projection excludes _id and the response builder copies only whitelisted fields — no _id leak (asserted in tests).
Frontend buyer-add-seller.tsx uses a 350ms debounce, cancels timers on unmount, and resets results/hasSearched when query becomes empty — correct behaviour for empty/whitespace q.

buyer-seller-details.tsx captures connectionStatus from the route param at mount (no refetch) and updates locally on send success or 409 — acceptable per the minimum-change spec; the duplicate-409 regex parses 'status: Pending|Accepted' to also reconcile race-conditions where another tab already created the connection.

The Card with onPress uses testID on an inner View; the actual Pressable wrapper is the parent (tabindex=0, cursor:pointer). On real mobile devices this is fine (touch bubbles to Pressable); on web-Playwright, clicking the testID div doesn't always fire the Pressable. Recommend assigning testID to a child of Card via accessibilityLabel/testID propagation or wrapping the Card in a Pressable that holds the testID directly.
Helpful empty-state distinguishes 'has not searched yet' (Start typing…) vs 'searched but no results' (No sellers found) — good UX.

success_rate.frontend: 9/9 verified flows pass — search input, debounce, empty-state, business-name partial match, lowercase code match, pincode match, card → details navigation, Send Connection Request transition to 'Request Pending', and persistence of Pending status on re-open.

seed_data_creation: Created 1 buyer (mobile 9364546774, PIN 1234) and 1 seller (SELLER-1084, business 'UITEST_GreenLeaf_KMHHB', pincode 560038) for UI tests; pytest module also creates 1 buyer + 3 sellers per session via /api/auth/register. All accounts use mock OTP 123456 and prefix TEST/UITEST.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend search endpoint is at GET /api/buyer/sellers/search?q=... — reuse /app/backend/tests/test_buyer_seller_search.py for regression. UI testIDs in the new flow: search-seller-btn (Appbar action on (buyer)/home), add-seller-fab (FAB), seller-search-input, search-loading, search-result-<sellerCode>, chip-code-<userId>, chip-pincode-<userId>, chip-status-<userId>, empty-title, detail-business-name, detail-seller-code, detail-availability, detail-connection-status, detail-owner-name, detail-full-address, detail-pincode, detail-mobile, send-request-btn, connection-helper-text, back-btn. To click a search-result Card from Playwright on web, target an inner text node, e.g. `page.click('[data-testid="search-result-SELLER-XXXX"] >> text=<businessName>')`; clicking the bare testID div does not trigger the Pressable on web (works fine on real device).
rca_of_issue: N/A — feature addition test, no bug investigation required. Only notable behavioural observation: on the web preview Playwright must click a child text inside the search-result Card to trigger navigation because react-native-paper's Card renders the testID on an inner View while the Pressable wrapper is the parent. This is a web-only automation quirk; on Android/iOS the touch event bubbles to the Pressable correctly.
